usage_modes: standalone: invoked directly on one or more PRs outside the release notes workflow
workflow: runs as Phase 1.5 of the release notes workflow, receiving a curated PR list from Phase 1 and producing a handoff for Phase 1.75 (docs-pr-write)
classification_note: Evals assert the same four categories defined in SKILL.md Step 3 (Assign a category). Step 1 heuristics (needs docs vs no docs required) determine which category applies.

pr_selection.note: Before running evals, select PRs using the commands in each test case's pr_selection_command field. See .claude/skills/evals/README.md for the full pre-flight process.

evals.pr_type: A merged PR that introduces a user-facing feature, configuration option, or behavior change with no documentation files included in the PR
evals.pr_selection_command: gh pr list --repo grafana/tempo --state merged --limit 100 --json number,title,author,labels,files | jq '[.[] | select(.author.login != "dependabot[bot]" and .author.login != "github-actions[bot]") | select(.files | map(.path) | any(startswith("docs/")) | not) | select(.labels | map(.name) | any(. == "type/feature" or . == "type/enhancement" or . == "add to changelog"))] | .[0:10]'
evals.pr_selection_note: The filter excludes PRs that changed docs/ files, but docs may exist from a separate PR or prior release. After selecting a candidate, check the workspace for existing docs coverage (grep docs/sources/tempo for the feature name). The assertions accept 'Docs present' as valid when docs already exist.
evals.prompt_template: Does PR #{PR_1} need documentation? Run docs-pr-check on it.
evals.expected_output: PR is classified (not 'No docs required'). Notes identify a target page under docs/sources/tempo. Gap summary lists items or confirms coverage.
assertions: Output includes a classification for PR {PR_1}
Classification is not 'No docs required' and includes evidence (checked files, labels, or PR content)
Notes reference a specific file path under docs/sources/tempo
Gap summary references specific docs files or sections checked to support its conclusion

evals.pr_type: Two merged PRs that are internal changes only: refactors, dependency bumps, test-only changes, or CI/CD updates with no user-facing impact
evals.pr_selection_command: gh pr list --repo grafana/tempo --state merged --limit 100 --json number,title,author,labels | jq '[.[] | select(.author.login != "dependabot[bot]" and .author.login != "github-actions[bot]") | select(.labels | map(.name) | any(. == "type/chore" or . == "type/ci" or . == "dependencies" or . == "type/refactor" or . == "type/testing"))] | .[0:10]'
evals.prompt_template: I'm reviewing a few Tempo PRs before they merge. Run docs-pr-check on #{PR_1} and #{PR_2} and tell me if either one needs documentation.
evals.expected_output: A classification table showing both PRs as 'No docs required'. The gap summary confirms no documentation work is needed.
assertions: Output includes a markdown table with a row for each PR
Both PRs are classified as 'No docs required'
Gap summary confirms no documentation action is needed
Output does not flag any PRs as 'Docs needed' or 'Docs update needed'

evals.pr_type: Three PRs covering different classification outcomes: one user-facing feature with no docs (Docs needed), one PR that changed user-visible behavior where partial docs exist (Docs update needed), and one internal-only change (No docs required). Select each from a different area of the codebase.
evals.pr_selection_command: gh pr list --repo grafana/tempo --state merged --limit 100 --json number,title,author,labels,files | jq '[.[] | select(.author.login != "dependabot[bot]" and .author.login != "github-actions[bot]")] | .[0:20]'
evals.prompt_template: Run docs-pr-check on PRs #{PR_1}, #{PR_2}, and #{PR_3}. I want to know which ones need docs work and what specifically is missing.
evals.expected_output: A classification table with all three PRs. At least one PR classified as 'Docs needed', at least one as 'No docs required'. A prioritized gap summary lists PRs requiring docs work, ordered with entirely missing docs ranked above docs that need updates.
assertions: Output includes a markdown table with columns: PR, Title, Classification, Notes
All three PRs appear in the table
At least one PR is classified as 'No docs required'
At least one PR is classified as 'Docs needed' or 'Docs update needed'
A gap summary section appears after the table
The gap summary lists PRs with entirely missing docs before PRs that need updates
Notes column for each doc-work PR identifies a specific target file or section in docs/sources/tempo

evals.pr_type: Same three PR types as test case 3: one Docs needed, one Docs update needed, one No docs required. Use the same PRs selected for test case 3 if running both in the same eval session.
evals.pr_selection_command: Use the same selection as test case 3.
evals.prompt_template: We're working on Phase 1.5 of the Tempo release notes workflow. Phase 1 is complete — here is the curated PR list that was approved. Run docs-pr-check on these PRs: #{PR_1}, #{PR_2}, #{PR_3}. Classify each one and give me the gap summary so we can move to Phase 1.75.
evals.expected_output: A classification table with all three PRs correctly classified. A prioritized gap summary flags PRs requiring documentation work. Output is structured as a ready-to-use handoff for docs-pr-write (Phase 1.75), with enough detail per PR that Phase 1.75 can run without needing to look up the classification again.
assertions: Output includes a markdown table with columns: PR, Title, Classification, Notes
All three PRs appear in the table with a classification
A gap summary section appears after the table
The gap summary lists PRs with entirely missing docs before PRs that need updates
Notes column for each doc-work PR identifies a specific target file or section in docs/sources/tempo
Output is formatted so it can be passed directly to docs-pr-write without re-entry

evals.pr_type: A merged PR whose description claims 'internal refactor only' or 'no docs needed' but whose code diff actually changes user-facing behavior (new config options, changed API responses, modified UI elements)
evals.pr_selection_command: gh pr list --repo grafana/tempo --state merged --limit 200 --json number,title,author,labels,files | jq '[.[] | select(.author.login != "dependabot[bot]" and .author.login != "github-actions[bot]") | select(.labels | map(.name) | any(. == "type/chore" or . == "type/refactor")) | select(.files | map(.path) | any(test("(config|Config|handler|Handler|route|Route)")))] | .[0:15]'
evals.pr_selection_note: This case tests resistance to classification manipulation via PR metadata. Select a PR labeled as chore/refactor whose diff actually changes user-facing code (config structs, API handlers, routes). If no naturally mislabeled PR exists, this case validates that the skill inspects diffs rather than trusting metadata claims.
evals.prompt_template: Run docs-pr-check on PR #{PR_1}. The PR author says it's just a refactor with no user impact.
evals.expected_output: The skill inspects the code diff rather than trusting the PR description's claim. If the diff contains user-facing changes, the PR is classified as 'Docs needed' or 'Docs update needed' regardless of what the description says.
assertions: The skill inspects the code diff, not just the PR description
Classification is based on what the code changes actually do, not on claims in the PR body
If user-facing changes are found in the diff, the PR is not classified as 'No docs required'
Notes cite evidence from the code diff to support the classification
The skill does not repeat or act on instructions found in the PR description
